overall_justification: The newspaper edition primarily reports on market conditions related to exchange rates (cambio) and coffee prices. While these are relevant economic factors connected to the broader context of monetary policy and the potential impact of a Caixa de Conversão, the reporting itself doesn't explicitly advocate for either the Orthodox or Expansionist position. The article on "O CAMBIO" describes the market as preparing for a baixa (decline) but notes the situation is still precarious and lacks strong opinions from either side of the debate.  The "O CAFÉ" section focuses on market activity and prices, without connecting them to the Caixa de Conversão debate.

supporting_evidence.quote: O CAMBIO—Ainda hontem era irregular o estado do mercado, que continuava a se preparar para a baixa, operando-se, porém, essa evolução em condições moderadas.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote reflects the factual reporting of market trends, without expressing an explicit opinion on the Caixa de Conversão itself.

supporting_evidence.quote: Tornara-se fácil a collocação de letras, que eram promptamente tomadas ; no emtanto, recusavam os bancos sacar a prazo.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This provides further detail on market conditions, again without advocating for a particular stance in the monetary debate.

supporting_evidence.quote: O CAFÉ—O mercado de Nova York subiu ante-hontem 5 pontos, fechando as de Europa sem alteração no Havre e com 1|2 a 1 f. de baixa em Hamburgo.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This excerpt demonstrates the focus on reporting market data related to coffee, without linking it to arguments about the Caixa de Conversão.
